RAL.headline: Nasdaq 100 Dips On Hot CPI, Sandisk And Micron Tumble 9%: Stock Market Today
RAL.description: U.S. equities fell sharply on May 12, 2026, after April's CPI came in hotter than expected at 3.8%, raising concerns the Federal Reserve will keep rates on hold throughout the year. The Nasdaq 100 sank 2.1% as the semiconductor sector collapsed, with memory chip stocks hit particularly hard by South Korea's proposed 'AI Citizen Dividend.' Notable gainers included Zebra Technologies (+18%) and Wendy's (+15%), while ZoomInfo plummeted 33% on slashed guidance.

WEN.headline: Nasdaq 100 Dips On Hot CPI, Sandisk And Micron Tumble 9%: Stock Market Today
WEN.description: U.S. equities fell sharply on May 12, 2026, after April's CPI came in hotter than expected at 3.8%, raising concerns the Federal Reserve will keep rates on hold throughout the year. The Nasdaq 100 sank 2.1% as the semiconductor sector collapsed, with memory chip stocks hit particularly hard by South Korea's proposed 'AI Citizen Dividend.' Notable gainers included Zebra Technologies (+18%) and Wendy's (+15%), while ZoomInfo plummeted 33% on slashed guidance.

WEN.headline: Why Is Wendy's Stock Surging Over 14% In Tuesday Pre-Market?
WEN.description: Wendy's stock surged 14% in pre-market trading after reports that Nelson Peltz's Trian Fund Management is seeking financing for a potential takeover bid to privatize the fast-food chain. However, no formal acquisition approach has been made. The surge comes despite Wendy's facing significant challenges, including a 45% stock decline over the past year, shrinking U.S. store count, declining sales traffic, and a JPMorgan downgrade to 'Underweight' with a price target cut to $6.

QUBT.headline: Quantum Computing Stock Spikes On Q1 Results: What To Know
QUBT.description: Quantum Computing Inc. (QUBT) beat Street estimates in Q1 2026, reporting losses of $0.02 per share versus expected $0.05 loss, and revenue of $3.69 million exceeding the $3.28 million consensus by 12.7%. The strong year-over-year revenue growth was driven by acquisitions of LSI and NuCrypt. The stock surged 16.40% to $11.85 in extended trading.

QUBT.headline: Quantum Computing Inc. Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
QUBT.description: Quantum Computing Inc. (NASDAQ:QUBT) reported Q1 2026 revenues of $3.7 million, up significantly from $39 thousand in Q1 2025, driven by acquisitions of Luminar Semiconductor and NuCrypt. The company reported a net loss of $4.1 million compared to net income of $17.0 million in the prior year, primarily due to lower derivative liability gains and higher operating expenses. QCi ended the quarter with $1.4 billion in cash and investments and a $16 million contract backlog.

RKLB.headline: SpaceX IPO Could Put Weak Space Stocks 'Into Obscurity,' Rocket Lab CFO Says
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab's CFO Adam Spice warns that a potential SpaceX IPO in mid-2026 could create a market divide in space stocks, where high-quality companies with real infrastructure and recurring contracts benefit while weaker players face obscurity. SpaceX's entry into public markets would establish a clear benchmark for the sector, forcing investors to discriminate between legitimate space operators and companies relying on promotional narratives.

RKLB.headline: Rocket Lab Just Hit a New All-Time High—Time to Buy or Let It Breathe?
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab (RKLB) surged over 40% to an all-time high of $117.35 following strong Q1 2026 earnings with record $200.3M revenue and a $2.2B backlog. The company announced major contracts including its largest-ever launch deal, a $30M hypersonic contract, and the acquisition of Motiv Space Systems. While analysts raised price targets to $120 (Street-high), the stock appears overbought with RSI in the 70s and trading 20% above consensus targets, suggesting consolidation may be prudent before furthe

RKLB.headline: Stock Market Today, May 11: Markets Inch Upwards Even As Oil Prices Rise
RKLB.description: U.S. stock markets rose modestly on May 11, 2026, with tech and AI stocks driving gains despite rising oil prices and inflation concerns. The S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow all gained around 0.2%, fueled by semiconductor and AI rallies. However, surging crude oil prices near $99/barrel pressured airlines and rekindled inflation worries, while investment banks predict the Federal Reserve may delay rate cuts until 2026.

RKLB.headline: President Trump's Golden Dome Missile Shield Just Got $3.2 Billion Bigger
RKLB.description: The U.S. Space Force awarded $3.2 billion in contracts to 12 space companies to build space-based interceptors for the 'Golden Dome' missile defense system. The program aims to create a next-generation defense shield against ballistic, hypersonic, and cruise missiles. Major defense contractors including SpaceX, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and RTX Corporation are expected to be significant winners, with Lockheed Martin highlighted as the most attractive investment option based on valuation

RKLB.headline: Rocket Lab Stock Gains Bullish Backing: Analyst Sees A SpaceX Challenger
RKLB.description: Needham raised its price target on Rocket Lab (RKLB) to $120 from $95, maintaining a Buy rating after the company beat Q1 expectations with $200.4M in revenue (63% YoY growth) and a $2.2B backlog. The analyst highlighted progress on the Neutron rocket test launch scheduled for Q4 2026 and noted Rocket Lab's vertical integration positions it to challenge SpaceX. The stock surged 13.13% to a new 52-week high.

POET.headline: ROSEN, A LEADING INVESTOR RIGHTS LAW FIRM, Encourages POET Technologies Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action First Filed by the Firm – POET
POET.description: Rosen Law Firm is urging POET Technologies Inc. investors who purchased securities between April 1-27, 2026 to join a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that POET made false statements regarding its tax status as a potential passive foreign investment company (PFIC), failed to disclose negative tax implications for U.S. shareholders, and that a defendant violated a non-disclosure agreement by discussing business agreements publicly. The lead plaintiff deadline is June 29, 2026.

IONQ.headline: Quantum Computing Stocks IonQ and D-Wave Quantum Have Nearly Doubled in 5 Weeks -- Don't Take the Bait
IONQ.description: IonQ and D-Wave Quantum have surged 98% and 84% respectively since March 30, driven by excitement over quantum computing's growth prospects. However, the author warns investors against chasing this rally, citing concerns about unsustainable valuations (P/S ratios of 116 and 311), the technology's early adoption stage, and potential competition from larger tech companies with stronger balance sheets.

IONQ.headline: Stock Market Today, May 11: IonQ Rises as SkyWater Vote Advances Semiconductor Manufacturing Deal
IONQ.description: IonQ stock surged 15.75% after SkyWater Technology shareholders approved their merger agreement. The acquisition is expected to close in Q2-Q3 2026 and will give IonQ greater control over semiconductor fabrication and packaging capabilities through SkyWater's facilities in Minnesota, Florida, and Texas. Quantum computing peers D-Wave and Rigetti also gained on broader sector enthusiasm.

IONQ.headline: What's Going On With IonQ Stock Monday?
IONQ.description: IonQ shares surged 15.25% on Monday following strong Q1 2026 earnings with 755% year-over-year revenue growth to $64.7 million and raised full-year guidance to $260-270 million. The company is advancing its acquisition of SkyWater Technology, expected to close in Q2-Q3 2026, and is supporting a sustainable urban planning initiative. Short interest increased to 23.65% of public float, while technical analysis shows strong upward momentum with key support at $52.50.

MU.headline: Prediction: This Unstoppable Vanguard Index Fund Will Crush the S&P 500 (Again) in 2026
MU.description: The Vanguard S&P 500 Growth ETF (VOOG) is predicted to outperform the S&P 500 in 2026, with a 10% return year-to-date compared to the S&P 500's 8%. The Growth index's heavy weighting in technology stocks (48.1% vs 32.9% in the S&P 500) and exposure to AI infrastructure companies like Nvidia and Broadcom positions it for continued outperformance as geopolitical tensions ease and the AI industry continues to grow.

MU.headline: SanDisk, Micron Stocks Plummet As Korea Shockwave Batters The Great Memory Boom
MU.description: Memory and semiconductor stocks experienced a sharp selloff after a top South Korean official proposed redistributing AI profits to citizens. The Roundhill Memory ETF collapsed 11.8%, while SanDisk and Micron fell over 10%. The proposal raised concerns about whether excess AI infrastructure profits should flow entirely to shareholders or be shared with the public, exposing how leveraged the memory trade had become.

MU.headline: Nasdaq 100 Dips On Hot CPI, Sandisk And Micron Tumble 9%: Stock Market Today
MU.description: U.S. equities fell sharply on May 12, 2026, after April's CPI came in hotter than expected at 3.8%, raising concerns the Federal Reserve will keep rates on hold throughout the year. The Nasdaq 100 sank 2.1% as the semiconductor sector collapsed, with memory chip stocks hit particularly hard by South Korea's proposed 'AI Citizen Dividend.' Notable gainers included Zebra Technologies (+18%) and Wendy's (+15%), while ZoomInfo plummeted 33% on slashed guidance.

MU.headline: Why the Nasdaq-100 Fell Nearly 2% on Tuesday
MU.description: The Nasdaq-100 fell 1.9% on Tuesday, significantly outpacing losses in the S&P 500 and Dow Jones, driven by macroeconomic headwinds including higher-than-expected inflation, blocked oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, and geopolitical tensions. Major tech stocks including Broadcom, Micron, Amazon, Tesla, and Intel led the decline despite some positive company-specific news.

MU.headline: Trump Wanted Warsh For Rate Cuts, He May Get A Hike Instead
MU.description: President Trump's pick for Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh expected aggressive rate cuts, but April's inflation data showing 3.8% headline CPI and 2.8% core inflation has reversed market expectations. Fed funds futures now price in a 51% probability of a rate hike by January 2027, with core services inflation particularly concerning. Markets sold off sharply, with tech stocks hit hardest as higher-for-longer interest rates become the new reality.

MU.headline: Will S&P 500 Open Up Or Down On May 12?
MU.description: The S&P 500 closed at a record high on Monday but Polymarket traders predict a weaker opening on Tuesday as investors await April's Consumer Price Index report, which is expected to show the highest inflation print since September 2023. Geopolitical tensions with Iran and Middle East developments are also weighing on sentiment, though technology stocks remain resilient with strong earnings and AI enthusiasm supporting the market.

MU.headline: Up 377%, Is Intel Proving Why It Was a Mistake for Nvidia to Replace Intel in the Dow Jones Industrial Average?
MU.description: Intel has surged 377% since being removed from the Dow Jones Industrial Average in November 2024, significantly outperforming Nvidia's 46% gain since its addition. Intel's turnaround is driven by strong demand for CPUs and custom AI accelerators in the AI inference market, solid partnerships with hyperscalers like Alphabet, and improved financial performance. However, the article concludes that Nvidia remains the more deserving Dow component due to its broader AI ecosystem positioning and signif

MU.headline: Stock Market Today, May 11: Markets Inch Upwards Even As Oil Prices Rise
MU.description: U.S. stock markets rose modestly on May 11, 2026, with tech and AI stocks driving gains despite rising oil prices and inflation concerns. The S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow all gained around 0.2%, fueled by semiconductor and AI rallies. However, surging crude oil prices near $99/barrel pressured airlines and rekindled inflation worries, while investment banks predict the Federal Reserve may delay rate cuts until 2026.

MU.headline: Why Sandisk (Not Micron) Could Be the Biggest Winner Of the AI Memory Era
MU.description: Both Sandisk and Micron have benefited from the AI memory boom, with Sandisk up 560% and Micron up 180% year-to-date. However, Sandisk emerges as the more compelling investment due to its multi-year customer contracts, debt-free balance sheet, $6 billion buyback program, and lower capital intensity compared to Micron's aggressive $25+ billion capex spending. While both stocks carry cyclical risk, Sandisk's business model offers better cycle protection.

MU.headline: Industries: Mag-7 Isn’t the Only AI Game in Town
MU.description: While the Magnificent-7 tech giants have dominated the AI trade since ChatGPT's launch, investor focus is broadening to semiconductor and component suppliers benefiting from massive AI infrastructure spending. The AI-11 semiconductor stocks have outperformed the Mag-7 this year, as hyperscalers' capital expenditure drives demand across the entire AI supply chain from foundries to memory chips to storage.

LITE.headline: Small-Cap ETF Showdown: Schwab's SCHA vs. iShares' IJR
LITE.description: The Schwab U.S. Small-Cap ETF (SCHA) and iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF (IJR) offer different approaches to small-cap investing. SCHA provides broader diversification with 1,721 holdings and a lower 0.04% expense ratio, delivering higher 1-year returns (44% vs 37.1%), but carries higher volatility. IJR offers a more selective portfolio of 640 quality-screened holdings with lower risk and higher liquidity ($102.9B AUM), making it suitable for risk-conscious investors willing to pay a slightly hig

CRCL.headline: Circle IPO Math Looks Richer After Arc Draws BlackRock and A16z
CRCL.description: Circle's stock surged 15% after securing $222M in presale funding for Arc, its new Layer-1 blockchain for institutional stablecoin settlement, valued at $3B. The round attracted major institutional investors including BlackRock, Andreessen Horowitz, and others. Circle's corporate valuation is anticipated around $4B-$5B ahead of its IPO. BlackRock's BUIDL fund, which has grown to $3.2B in AUM, relies on Circle's USDC smart contract for 24/7 redemptions, with Circle generating $150M in protocol fe

CRCL.headline: Is Circle Internet Group Stock Due for a Big Rally After This Positive Crypto News?
CRCL.description: Circle Internet Group's stock rallied 15.91% following positive developments around the Clarity Act, which will allow stablecoin rewards for non-passive activities. The compromise eases concerns about a complete ban on stablecoin yields. Circle's USDC stablecoin saw 28% year-over-year growth and revenue rose 20% to $694 million. However, the stock remains down 56% from its 52-week high, and analysts recommend cautious positioning given ongoing regulatory uncertainty.

CRCL.headline: My Top 2 Recent Tech IPOs to Buy for May 2026
CRCL.description: Circle and CoreWeave are highlighted as two strong tech IPOs worth buying in May 2026. Circle, which went public in June 2025, has quadrupled from its $31 IPO price and issues USD Coin stablecoin with growing reserve interest income. CoreWeave, which went public in March 2025, has nearly tripled from its $40 IPO price and operates 43 AI data centers with 250,000+ GPUs, showing massive growth potential with a $99.4 billion backlog.
